commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=445d0370f07971e2f6ea91004808fbd7bae96a6c)
commands.review_summary:
This PR broadens `doctor.memory.status`'s local short-term memory path regex to count top-level date files with a four-digit timestamp suffix.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current `doctor.memory.status` filters recall-store entries through a strict date-only matcher, so a promoted entry with `memory/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md` is skipped before the promoted counts are calculated. I did not run a full live gateway repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-visible after-fix proof and a small code/test revision; it should not enter an automated repair lane while the external proof gate is missing.

target.title: fix(doctor): match short-term memory paths with timestamp suffixes
target.body:
## What

Fix `isShortTermMemoryPath()` in `doctor.memory.status` to also match daily memory files with timestamp suffixes (e.g. `memory/2026-05-28-1503.md`).

## Root Cause

The regex `/(?:^|\/)memory\/(\d{4})-(\d{2})-(\d{2})\.md$/` only matched bare date filenames (e.g. `memory/2026-05-28.md`), skipping the 226 of 512 recall-store entries that use timestamp-suffixed paths.

## Fix

Add optional `(?:-\d{4})?` suffix to both the path regex and the basename regex, aligning with the memory-core extension's existing `SHORT_TERM_PATH_RE` which already allows `(?:-[^/]+)?`.

## Impact

- `promotedToday` now correctly counts timestamp-suffixed promotions
- `shortTermCount` and `promotedTotal` values are accurate
- Actual dreaming functionality was unaffected — only the status/UI reporting

Closes #90896
